name: popover-wire
description: The server-data-driven variant of the popover overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): open-state lives in typed Java and the panel CONTENT is (re)derived from server state on each open (gest's calendar-filter feed). The server re-renders the conditionally-rendered panel, the client morphs it; no <slot>, no @floating-ui/dom, no Lit. Positioning stays the server-pure CSS Anchor Positioning the popover PARTIAL uses. Use the popover JTE partial for a static popover; this heavier WIRE tier is for server-held content + open-state.
docs: Register PopoverComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / @template.lievit.popover-wire(...); put your server-derived panel body in the owned data-popover-panel region (NOT a slot), or derive it from a @Wire field. The trigger fires `toggle` over the wire; `show`/`close` are also exposed. The panel is conditionally rendered (@if(open)) so its content cannot fail to project. The @LievitComponent(template="lievit/popover-wire") name + the io.lievit.wire package are yours to change once copied.
